NKJV.1: A Meditation of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning the words of Cush, a Benjamite. O LORD my God, in You I put my trust; Save me from all those who persecute me; And deliver me,
NKJV.2: Lest they tear me like a lion, Rending me in pieces, while there is none to deliver.
NKJV.3: O LORD my God, if I have done this: If there is iniquity in my hands,
NKJV.4: If I have repaid evil to him who was at peace with me, Or have plundered my enemy without cause,
NKJV.5: Let the enemy pursue me and overtake me; Yes, let him trample my life to the earth, And lay my honor in the dust. Selah
NKJV.6: Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; Lift Yourself up because of the rage of my enemies; Rise up for me to the judgment You have commanded!
NKJV.7: So the congregation of the peoples shall surround You; For their sakes, therefore, return on high.
NKJV.8: The LORD shall judge the peoples;  Judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, And according to my integrity within me.
NKJV.9: Oh, let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, But establish the just;  For the righteous God tests the hearts and minds.
NKJV.10: My defense is of God, Who saves the upright in heart.
NKJV.11: God is a just judge, And God is angry with the wicked every day.
NKJV.12: If he does not turn back, He will sharpen His sword; He bends His bow and makes it ready.
NKJV.13: He also prepares for Himself instruments of death; He makes His arrows into fiery shafts.
NKJV.14: Behold, the wicked brings forth iniquity; Yes, he conceives trouble and brings forth falsehood.
NKJV.15: He made a pit and dug it out, And has fallen into the ditch which he made.
NKJV.16: His trouble shall return upon his own head, And his violent dealing shall come down on his own crown.
NKJV.17: I will praise the LORD according to His righteousness, And will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

KJV.1: Shiggaion<S>7692</S> of David<S>1732</S>, which he sang<S>7891</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, concerning the words<S>1697</S> of Cush<S>3568</S> the Benjamite<S>1121</S> <S>1145</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>, in thee do I put my trust<S>2620</S>: save<S>3467</S> me from all them that persecute<S>7291</S> me, and deliver<S>5337</S> me: <sup>words: or, business</sup>

KJV.4: If I have rewarded<S>1580</S> evil<S>7451</S> unto him that was at peace<S>7999</S> with me; (yea, I have delivered<S>2502</S> him that without cause<S>7387</S> is mine enemy<S>6887</S>:)
KJV.5: Let the enemy<S>341</S> persecute<S>7291</S> my soul<S>5315</S>, and take<S>5381</S> it; yea, let him tread down<S>7429</S> my life<S>2416</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>, and lay<S>7931</S> mine honour<S>3519</S> in the dust<S>6083</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.6: Arise<S>6965</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, in thine anger<S>639</S>, lift up<S>5375</S> thyself because of the rage<S>5678</S> of mine enemies<S>6887</S>: and awake<S>5782</S> for me to the judgment<S>4941</S> that thou hast commanded<S>6680</S>.
KJV.7: So shall the congregation<S>5712</S> of the people<S>3816</S> compass thee about<S>5437</S>: for their sakes therefore return<S>7725</S> thou on high<S>4791</S>.
KJV.8: The LORD<S>3068</S> shall judge<S>1777</S> the people<S>5971</S>: judge<S>8199</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S>, according to my righteousness<S>6664</S>, and according to mine integrity<S>8537</S> that is in me.
KJV.9: Oh let the wickedness<S>7451</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> come to an end<S>1584</S>; but establish<S>3559</S> the just<S>6662</S>: for the righteous<S>6662</S> God<S>430</S> trieth<S>974</S> the hearts<S>3826</S> and reins<S>3629</S>.
KJV.10: My defence<S>4043</S> is of God<S>430</S>, which saveth<S>3467</S> the upright<S>3477</S> in heart<S>3820</S>. <sup>My: Heb. My buckler is upon God</sup>
KJV.11: God<S>430</S> judgeth<S>8199</S> the righteous<S>6662</S>, and God<S>410</S> is angry<S>2194</S> with the wicked every day<S>3117</S>. <sup>judgeth: or, is a righteous judge</sup>
KJV.12: If he turn<S>7725</S> not, he will whet<S>3913</S> his sword<S>2719</S>; he hath bent<S>1869</S> his bow<S>7198</S>, and made it ready<S>3559</S>.
KJV.13: He hath also prepared<S>3559</S> for him the instruments<S>3627</S> of death<S>4194</S>; he ordaineth<S>6466</S> his arrows<S>2671</S> against the persecutors<S>1814</S>.
KJV.14: Behold, he travaileth<S>2254</S> with iniquity<S>205</S>, and hath conceived<S>2029</S> mischief<S>5999</S>, and brought forth<S>3205</S> falsehood<S>8267</S>.
KJV.15: He made<S>3738</S> a pit<S>953</S>, and digged<S>2658</S> it, and is fallen<S>5307</S> into the ditch<S>7845</S> which he made<S>6466</S>. <sup>He made a pit: Heb. He hath digged a pit</sup>
KJV.16: His mischief<S>5999</S> shall return<S>7725</S> upon his own head<S>7218</S>, and his violent<S>2555</S> dealing shall come down<S>3381</S> upon his own pate<S>6936</S>.

ESV.1: O LORD my God, in you do I take refuge; save me from all my pursuers and deliver me,
ESV.2: lest like a lion they tear my soul apart, rending it in pieces, with none to deliver.
ESV.3: O LORD my God, if I have done this, if there is wrong in my hands,
ESV.4: if I have repaid my friend  with evil or plundered my enemy without cause,
ESV.5: let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and let him trample my life to the ground and lay my glory in the dust. Selah
ESV.6: Arise, O LORD, in your anger; lift yourself up against the fury of my enemies; awake for me; you have appointed a judgment.
ESV.7: Let the assembly of the peoples be gathered about you; over it return on high.
ESV.8: The LORD judges the peoples; judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and according to the integrity that is in me.
ESV.9: Oh, let the evil of the wicked come to an end, and may you establish the righteous — you who test the minds and hearts,  O righteous God!
ESV.10: My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.
ESV.11: God is a righteous judge, and a God who feels indignation every day.
ESV.12: If a man  does not repent, God  will whet his sword; he has bent and readied his bow;
ESV.13: he has prepared for him his deadly weapons, making his arrows fiery shafts.
ESV.14: Behold, the wicked man conceives evil and is pregnant with mischief and gives birth to lies.
ESV.15: He makes a pit, digging it out, and falls into the hole that he has made.
ESV.16: His mischief returns upon his own head, and on his own skull his violence descends.
ESV.17: I will give to the LORD the thanks due to his righteousness, and I will sing praise to the name of the LORD, the Most High.

NASB.1: A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, a Benjamite. O LORD my God, in You I have taken refuge; Save me from all those who pursue me, and deliver me,
NASB.2: Or he will tear my soul like a lion, Dragging me away, while there is none to deliver.
NASB.3: O LORD my God, if I have done this, If there is injustice in my hands,
NASB.4: If I have rewarded evil to my friend, Or have plundered him who without cause was my adversary,
NASB.5: Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake [it]; And let him trample my life down to the ground And lay my glory in the dust. Selah.
NASB.6: Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; Lift up Yourself against the rage of my adversaries, And arouse Yourself for me; You have appointed judgment.
NASB.7: Let the assembly of the peoples encompass You, And over them return on high.
NASB.8: The LORD judges the peoples; Vindicate me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and my integrity that is in me.
NASB.9: O let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; For the righteous God tries the hearts and minds.
NASB.10: My shield is with God, Who saves the upright in heart.
NASB.11: God is a righteous judge, And a God who has indignation every day.
NASB.12: If a man does not repent, He will sharpen His sword; He has bent His bow and made it ready.
NASB.13: He has also prepared for Himself deadly weapons; He makes His arrows fiery shafts.
NASB.14: Behold, he travails with wickedness, And he conceives mischief and brings forth falsehood.
NASB.15: He has dug a pit and hollowed it out, And has fallen into the hole which he made.
NASB.16: His mischief will return upon his own head, And his violence will descend upon his own pate.
NASB.17: I will give thanks to the LORD according to His righteousness And will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

NLT.1:
I come to you for protection, O LORD my God.
Save me from my persecutors — rescue me!
NLT.2:
If you don’t, they will maul me like a lion,
tearing me to pieces with no one to rescue me.
NLT.3:
O LORD my God, if I have done wrong
or am guilty of injustice,
NLT.4:
if I have betrayed a friend
or plundered my enemy without cause,
NLT.5:
then let my enemies capture me.
Let them trample me into the ground
and drag my honor in the dust. Interlude
NLT.6:
Arise, O LORD, in anger!
Stand up against the fury of my enemies!
Wake up, my God, and bring justice!
NLT.7:
Gather the nations before you.
Rule over them from on high.
NLT.8:
The LORD judges the nations.
Declare me righteous, O LORD,
for I am innocent, O Most High!
NLT.9:
End the evil of those who are wicked,
and defend the righteous.
For you look deep within the mind and heart,
O righteous God.
NLT.10:
God is my shield,
saving those whose hearts are true and right.
NLT.11:
God is an honest judge.
He is angry with the wicked every day.
NLT.12:
If a person does not repent,
God will sharpen his sword;
he will bend and string his bow.
NLT.13:
He will prepare his deadly weapons
and shoot his flaming arrows.
NLT.14:
The wicked conceive evil;
they are pregnant with trouble
and give birth to lies.
NLT.15:
They dig a deep pit to trap others,
then fall into it themselves.
NLT.16:
The trouble they make for others backfires on them.
The violence they plan falls on their own heads.
NLT.17:
I will thank the LORD because he is just;
I will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

WEB.1: A meditation by David, which he sang to Yahweh, concerning the words of Cush, the Benjamite. Yahweh, my God, I take refuge in you. Save me from all those who pursue me, and deliver me,
WEB.2: lest they tear apart my soul like a lion, ripping it in pieces, while there is no one to deliver.
WEB.3: Yahweh, my God, if I have done this, if there is iniquity in my hands,
WEB.4: if I have rewarded evil to him who was at peace with me (yes, if I have delivered him who without cause was my adversary),
WEB.5: let the enemy pursue my soul, and overtake it; yes, let him tread my life down to the earth, and lay my glory in the dust. Selah.
WEB.6: Arise, Yahweh, in your anger. Lift up yourself against the rage of my adversaries. Awake for me. You have commanded judgment.
WEB.7: Let the congregation of the peoples surround you. Rule over them on high.
WEB.8: Yahweh administers judgment to the peoples. Judge me, Yahweh, according to my righteousness, and to my integrity that is in me.
WEB.9: Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; their minds and hearts are searched by the righteous God.
WEB.10: My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.
WEB.11: God is a righteous judge, yes, a God who has indignation every day.
WEB.12: If a man doesn’t relent, he will sharpen his sword; he has bent and strung his bow.
WEB.13: He has also prepared for himself the instruments of death. He makes ready his flaming arrows.
WEB.14: Behold,  he travails with iniquity. Yes, he has conceived mischief, and brought out falsehood.
WEB.15: He has dug a hole, and has fallen into the pit which he made.
WEB.16: The trouble he causes shall return to his own head. His violence shall come down on the crown of his own head.
WEB.17: I will give thanks to Yahweh according to his righteousness, and will sing praise to the name of Yahweh Most High.

AMP.1:
An Ode of David, [perhaps in a wild, irregular, enthusiastic strain,] which he sang to the LORD concerning the words of Cush, a Benjamite. O LORD my God, in You I take refuge;
Save me and rescue me from all those who pursue me,
AMP.2:
So that my enemy will not tear me like a lion,
Dragging me away while there is no one to rescue [me].
AMP.3:
O LORD my God, if I have done this,
If there is injustice in my hands,
AMP.4:
If I have done evil to him who was at peace with me,
Or without cause robbed him who was my enemy,
AMP.5:
Let the enemy pursue me and overtake me;
And let him trample my life to the ground
And lay my honor in the dust. Selah.
AMP.6:
Arise, O LORD, in Your anger;
Lift up Yourself against the rage of my enemies;
Rise up for me; You have commanded judgment and vindication.
AMP.7:
Let the assembly of the nations be gathered around You,
And return on high over them.
AMP.8:
The LORD judges the peoples;
Judge me, O LORD, and grant me justice according to my righteousness and according to the integrity within me.
AMP.9:
Oh, let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous [those in right standing with You];
For the righteous God tries the hearts and minds.
AMP.10:
My shield and my defense depend on God,
Who saves the upright in heart.
AMP.11:
God is a righteous judge,
And a God who is indignant every day.
AMP.12:
If a man does not repent, God will sharpen His sword;
He has strung and bent His [mighty] bow and made it ready.
AMP.13:
He has also prepared [other] deadly weapons for Himself;
He makes His arrows fiery shafts [aimed at the unrepentant].
AMP.14:
Behold, the [wicked and irreverent] man is pregnant with sin,
And he conceives mischief and gives birth to lies.
AMP.15:
He has dug a pit and hollowed it out,
And has fallen into the [very] pit which he made [as a trap].
AMP.16:
His mischief will return on his own head,
And his violence will come down on the top of his head [like loose dirt].
AMP.17:
I will give thanks to the LORD according to His righteousness and justice,
And I will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God! God! I am running to you for dear life;
the chase is wild.
MSG.2:
If they catch me, I'm finished:
ripped to shreds by foes fierce as lions,
dragged into the forest and left
unlooked for, unremembered.
MSG.3: God, if I've done what they say—
MSG.4:
betrayed my friends,
ripped off my enemies—
If my hands are really that dirty,
MSG.5:
let them get me, walk all over me,
leave me flat on my face in the dirt.
MSG.6:
Stand up, God; pit your holy fury
against my furious enemies.
Wake up, God.
MSG.7:
My accusers have packed
the courtroom; it's judgment time.
MSG.8:
Take your place on the bench, reach for your gavel,
throw out the false charges against me.
I'm ready, confident in your verdict:
"Innocent."
MSG.9:
Close the book on Evil, God,
but publish your mandate for us.
You get us ready for life:
you probe for our soft spots,
you knock off our rough edges.
MSG.10:
And I'm feeling so fit, so safe:
made right, kept right.
MSG.11:
God in solemn honor does things right,
but his nerves are sandpapered raw.
Nobody gets by with anything.
MSG.12:
God is already in action—
Sword honed on his whetstone,
bow strung, arrow on the string,
MSG.13:
Lethal weapons in hand,
each arrow a flaming missile.
MSG.14:
Look at that guy!
He had sex with sin,
he's pregnant with evil.
Oh, look! He's having
the baby—a Lie-Baby!
MSG.15:
See that man shoveling day after day,
digging, then concealing, his man-trap
down that lonely stretch of road?
Go back and look again—you'll see him in it headfirst,
legs waving in the breeze.
MSG.17:
I'm thanking God, who makes things right.
I'm singing the fame of heaven-high God.

NIV.1:
Psalm 7
A shiggaion of David, which he sang to the Lord concerning Cush, a Benjamite.
O Lord my God, I take refuge in you;
save and deliver me from all who pursue me,
NIV.2:
or they will tear me like a lion
and rip me to pieces with no one to rescue me.
NIV.3:
O Lord my God, if I have done this
and there is guilt on my hands—
NIV.4:
if I have done evil to him who is at peace with me
or without cause have robbed my foe—
NIV.5:
then let my enemy pursue and overtake me;
let him trample my life to the ground
and make me sleep in the dust. Selah
NIV.6:
Arise, O Lord, in your anger;
rise up against the rage of my enemies.
Awake, my God; decree justice.
NIV.7:
Let the assembled peoples gather around you.
Rule over them from on high;
NIV.8:
let the Lord judge the peoples.
Judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness,
according to my integrity, O Most High.
NIV.9:
O righteous God,
who searches minds and hearts,
bring to an end the violence of the wicked
and make the righteous secure.
NIV.10:
My shield is God Most High,
who saves the upright in heart.
NIV.11:
God is a righteous judge,
a God who expresses his wrath every day.
NIV.12:
If he does not relent,
he will sharpen his sword;
he will bend and string his bow.
NIV.13:
He has prepared his deadly weapons;
he makes ready his flaming arrows.
NIV.14:
He who is pregnant with evil
and conceives trouble gives birth to disillusionment.
NIV.15:
He who digs a hole and scoops it out
falls into the pit he has made.
NIV.16:
The trouble he causes recoils on himself;
his violence comes down on his own head.
NIV.17:
I will give thanks to the Lord because of his righteousness
and will sing praise to the name of the Lord Most High.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD my God, in you I take refuge;
save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me,
NRSVCE.2:
or like a lion they will tear me apart;
they will drag me away, with no one to rescue.
NRSVCE.3:
O LORD my God, if I have done this,
if there is wrong in my hands,
NRSVCE.4:
if I have repaid my ally with harm
or plundered my foe without cause,
NRSVCE.5:
then let the enemy pursue and overtake me,
trample my life to the ground,
and lay my soul in the dust. Selah
NRSVCE.6:
Rise up, O LORD, in your anger;
lift yourself up against the fury of my enemies;
awake, O my God; you have appointed a judgment.
NRSVCE.7:
Let the assembly of the peoples be gathered around you,
and over it take your seat on high.
NRSVCE.8:
The LORD judges the peoples;
judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness
and according to the integrity that is in me.
NRSVCE.9:
O let the evil of the wicked come to an end,
but establish the righteous,
you who test the minds and hearts,
O righteous God.
NRSVCE.10:
God is my shield,
who saves the upright in heart.
NRSVCE.11:
God is a righteous judge,
and a God who has indignation every day.
NRSVCE.12:
If one does not repent, God will whet his sword;
he has bent and strung his bow;
NRSVCE.13:
he has prepared his deadly weapons,
making his arrows fiery shafts.
NRSVCE.14:
See how they conceive evil,
and are pregnant with mischief,
and bring forth lies.
NRSVCE.15:
They make a pit, digging it out,
and fall into the hole that they have made.
NRSVCE.16:
Their mischief returns upon their own heads,
and on their own heads their violence descends.
NRSVCE.17:
I will give to the LORD the thanks due to his righteousness,
and sing praise to the name of the LORD, the Most High.

NET.1: O LORD my God, in you I have taken shelter. Deliver me from all who chase me! Rescue me!
NET.2: Otherwise they will rip me to shreds like a lion; they will tear me to bits and no one will be able to rescue me.
NET.3: O LORD my God, if I have done what they say, or am guilty of unjust actions,
NET.4: or have wronged my ally, or helped his lawless enemy,
NET.5: may an enemy relentlessly chase me and catch me; may he trample me to death and leave me lying dishonored in the dust. (Selah)
NET.6: Stand up angrily, LORD! Rise up with raging fury against my enemies! Wake up for my sake and execute the judgment you have decreed for them!
NET.7: The countries are assembled all around you; take once more your rightful place over them!
NET.8: The LORD judges the nations. Vindicate me, LORD, because I am innocent, because I am blameless, O Exalted One!
NET.9: May the evil deeds of the wicked come to an end! But make the innocent secure, O righteous God, you who examine inner thoughts and motives!
NET.10: The Exalted God is my shield, the one who delivers the morally upright.
NET.11: God is a just judge; he is angry throughout the day.
NET.12: If a person does not repent, God sharpens his sword and prepares to shoot his bow.
NET.13: He prepares to use deadly weapons against him; he gets ready to shoot flaming arrows.
NET.14: See the one who is pregnant with wickedness, who conceives destructive plans, and gives birth to harmful lies —
NET.15: he digs a pit and then falls into the hole he has made.
NET.16: He becomes the victim of his own destructive plans and the violence he intended for others falls on his own head.
NET.17: I will thank the LORD for his justice; I will sing praises to the sovereign LORD!
